chapter_context.summary: Exhorts backsliding Israel and treacherous Judah to return, promising shepherds after God's own heart, and detailing their future united confession of sin.

outline.text: Shame of Spiritual Adultery and Rebuff of Early Mercy
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Shame of Spiritual Adultery and Rebuff of Early Mercy (3:1-5), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

outline.text: Treacherous Judah Worse Than Backsliding Israel
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Treacherous Judah Worse Than Backsliding Israel (3:6-11), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

outline.text: Pleading for Israel's Return; Promising Shepherds and Union
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Pleading for Israel's Return; Promising Shepherds and Union (3:12-19), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

outline.text: Weeping Confession and Return of Covenant Children
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Weeping Confession and Return of Covenant Children (3:20-25), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Warning
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of warning, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Weeping Prophet
key_themes.description: Jeremiah's deep emotional connection to the message and the people, illustrating the heart of God breaking for His children's rebellion.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and historical accounts of Chapter 3, in what ways does Jeremiah's call to faithfulness encourage you in your own walk today?
How does this chapter prompt you to choose covenant obedience and trust in God's promises of restoration over temporary security?

cultural_historical.title: The Fall of Jerusalem and the Exile
cultural_historical.text: The historical backdrop of Jeremiah's ministry, witnessing the gradual collapse of Judah under the Babylonian Empire and the destruction of the Temple.
